title: EEOC sues iTutorGroup over age-discriminatory automated tutor-screening software
harm_types: discrimination or unequal treatment; financial loss; institutional accountability harm
why_this_posture_now: The local package already records alleged automated rejection of more than 200 qualified applicants plus later settlement/decree terms, so the public record points to realized adverse treatment rather than a mere latent risk.
what_blocks_stronger_use: The local package still lacks captured complaint/decree/order text, so event coding should stay source-qualified and should not be presented as court-verified findings.
paper_safe_use_now: Use as one reviewer-safe example that CSET event posture can fit documented adverse-treatment rows even when the legal record remains agency-summary-bounded.
visualization_hook: Small methods appendix panel contrasting realized discrimination events with security near-miss rows.
next_smallest_move: If a court-facing complaint or decree enters the local package, re-check whether this row can move from medium-confidence manual overlay to stronger reusable coding guidance.

title: UK ICO orders Serco Leisure and associated trusts to stop biometric attendance monitoring
harm_types: privacy or data protection harm; institutional accountability harm; psychological or dignitary harm
why_this_posture_now: The local package has direct regulator notice text describing unlawful biometric attendance monitoring, cessation orders, and destruction requirements, which is the clearest current example of a realized public-source harm event rather than a hypothetical issue.
what_blocks_stronger_use: This posture should not be generalized into sector prevalence or broader legality conclusions beyond the ICO notice.
paper_safe_use_now: Use as the anchor row showing that the CSET event overlay can be stable when LIMEN already has direct authoritative document text and concrete remedial action.
visualization_hook: Evidence-tier funnel companion showing one document-grade event-coded row.
next_smallest_move: Keep this row as the benchmark comparator when testing coder agreement on weaker rows.

title: Italian DPA orders urgent provisional limitation on ChatGPT personal-data processing in Italy
harm_types: privacy or data protection harm; democratic, civic, or public-trust harm
why_this_posture_now: The order text supports a serious governance and rights concern, but the current local summary is cleaner for a documented issue/regulatory intervention posture than for a discrete realized injury event under a CSET-like overlay.
what_blocks_stronger_use: The package should preserve the regulator's own provisional-order framing and avoid converting process/privacy concerns into a stronger event claim than the source itself requires.
paper_safe_use_now: Use as a cautionary example that CSET issue posture may be more stable than event posture for governance-heavy regulator actions.
visualization_hook: Methods note sidebar on why some direct regulator actions still code better as issue than event.
next_smallest_move: Write a one-page coding memo defining when privacy/regulator rows count as issue versus event using only source-visible downstream effects.

title: LangChain LLMMathChain allowed prompt injection into Python exec, creating arbitrary code execution risk
harm_types: cybersecurity harm; privacy or data protection harm; uncertain or no realized harm but credible hazard
why_this_posture_now: The public CVE text describes a credible arbitrary-code-execution pathway but the local package does not document a realized downstream injury event, making near-miss the cleanest current CSET posture analog.
what_blocks_stronger_use: The lane should not infer exploitation prevalence or user harm from vulnerability presence alone.
paper_safe_use_now: Use as the anchor row showing why CSET near-miss can improve interpretation of authoritative security records without reclassifying them as realized incidents.
visualization_hook: Security-governance panel contrasting realized event rows with credible-hazard near-miss rows.
next_smallest_move: Test one or two additional authoritative security rows for agreement before using near-miss counts in any figure.

title: DOJ charges Nate founder over alleged AI investment-fraud misstatements and hidden human transaction processing
harm_types: financial loss; deception or fraud; institutional accountability harm
why_this_posture_now: The local package describes alleged investor-facing deception and hidden human transaction processing, which looks event-like on substance, but the record remains charging-stage and accusation-bounded.
what_blocks_stronger_use: Because the package lacks normalized indictment text and the matter is not adjudicated, this is the highest coder-drift row in the pilot and should not be counted without explicit accusation-stage caveats.
paper_safe_use_now: Use only as a negative-control example showing where CSET event posture becomes unstable under accusation-stage evidence and may be better left uncoded in main-text figures.
visualization_hook: Reviewer appendix row illustrating accusation-stage overlay risk.
next_smallest_move: Keep out of any headline chart unless the document layer improves or the coding memo explicitly defines accusation-stage handling.
